response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile individual contributor with nearly two decades of hands-on development and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, CI infrastructure, Selenium training, MCP code generators), but this is a Level II people-management role and the resume shows no people-management or formal team-leadership experience whatsoever. Technical leadership is implied only through a side-project founder role and workshops, not through owning roadmaps, performance management, or organizational change. The minimum requirement of 2 years people management is unmet, making the fit poor despite solid engineering depth.
strengths: Deep developer-tooling and testing background: authored test runners (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), built CI/dev-ops infrastructure, and created testing courses
19+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing inclination through workshops, dojos, and conference talks
concerns: No people-management experience, which is an explicit minimum requirement for this managerial role
No formal technical-leadership role setting team priorities, roadmaps, or performance expectations at organizational scale
Unexplained 8-month employment gap between January 2020 and September 2020

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure expertise
No formal technical leadership track record
